title: Sambalpur Declares School Holiday Till April 22 Amid Severe Heatwave - Pragativadi
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
full_text:
Sambalpur district administration announced holidays for all schools, colleges, and Anganwadi centres from April 20 to April 22 as the region continues to reel under an intense heatwave.
District Collector Siddheshwar Baliram Bondar made the decision after consulting the Education Department and other officials. He said the move aims to protect students from extreme heat as temperatures hover between 41°C and 42°C across the district.
Parents had expressed growing concern over schools remaining open despite the scorching conditions. The administration responded promptly, declaring the three-day closure and assuring that further extensions would be considered if temperatures rise.
Officials confirmed that the directive applies to all government and private educational institutions. Teachers and staff will continue administrative duties such as admissions and record updates while classes remain suspended.
The Collector emphasised that the administration will monitor weather conditions closely and take additional measures if necessary. Sambalpur joins other western Odisha districts that have announced similar closures following the India Meteorological Department’s warning of severe heatwave conditions.

title: Odisha sees first suspected heatstroke death amid rising temperatures - Odisha TV
full_text:
A youth died under suspicious circumstances in Odisha’s Sambalpur district on Saturday, with family members suspecting heatstroke as the possible cause of death. The deceased has been identified as Biswajit Sahu, a resident of Rasiapali village under Jhankarpali panchayat in the district.
According to reports, Biswajit had been working outdoors on the previous day amid prevailing heatwave conditions. After returning home, he reportedly complained of mild uneasiness and fatigue. However, his health condition deteriorated suddenly on Saturday morning, triggering panic among family members.
He was immediately rushed to the district headquarters hospital for treatment. However, doctors declared him dead upon arrival, leaving the family in shock. The sudden nature of his death has raised concerns, with relatives suspecting that prolonged exposure to extreme heat may have led to a heatstroke-related fatality.
Meanwhile, officials have urged caution against drawing conclusions before medical confirmation. The Chief District Medical Officer (CDMO) stated that the body will be sent for post-mortem examination to determine the exact cause of death.
“The precise reason behind the death will be clear only after the post-mortem report is received,” the official said.
The incident comes amid rising temperatures across the state, with authorities repeatedly issuing advisories urging people to avoid prolonged exposure to the sun and take necessary precautions.
Further investigation is underway, and more details are awaited.
